summary: Crisp sound‑board recording of a high‑energy ’73 show highlighted by an epic Weather Report Suite/Let It Grow, soaring Dark Star → Morning Dew and a smoking China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider.
review: The SBD capture is remarkably clear – crisp highs, solid bass definition and only occasional pops or the brief audience patch in the latter part of Morning Dew. Musically the concert shines throughout; the horn‑enhanced Weather Report Suite, a fiery Dark Star that builds into an emotional Morning Dew, plus tight performances of China Cat > Rider and Playing in the Band make this one of the stand‑out shows of 1973.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews overwhelmingly praise both performance (many 4‑5 stars) and sound quality; only minor technical quirks are noted, giving a combined show/recording rating well into the ‘good‑to‑great’ range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix with excellent instrument separation; occasional tiny pops/cracks and a short audience patch during the end of Morning Dew, but none detract from overall listenability.

standout_songs: Weather Report Suite > Let It Grow
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set unfolds naturally – a strong first set capped by China Cat → Rider, followed by a horn‑rich second set that builds through Weather Report into an expansive Dark Star and resolves beautifully with Morning Dew. The energy stays high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his expressive wah‑inflected solos, passionate vocals and tight lead work throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly highlighted for a thunderous, almost ‘over‑the‑top’ bass solo in Dark Star and overall energetic playing.
band_member_comments.Keys: Martin Fierro’s sax/horn contributions are singled out as a key element of the Weather Report Suite and the horn‑driven sections; Keith’s keyboard work is noted as solid but not a focus.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann receives accolades for “stellar” drumming, especially during Dark Star and the high‑energy jams.
